verses:
sucāru⋅vaktramaṇḍalaṁ
sukarṇa⋅ratnakuṇḍalam
sucarcitāṅga⋅candanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

sudīrgha⋅netrapaṅkajaṁ
śikhi⋅śikhaṇḍa⋅mūrdhajam
anaṅgakoṭi⋅mohanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

sunāsikāgra⋅mauktikaṁ
svacchanda⋅danta⋅paṅktikam
navāmvudāṅga⋅cikkaṇaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

kareṇa veṇurañjitaṁ
gati⋅karīndragañjitam
dukūla⋅pīta⋅śobhanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

tribhaṅga⋅deha⋅sundaraṁ
nakhadyuti⋅sudhākaram
amūlya⋅ratna⋅bhūṣaṇaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

sugandha⋅aṅgasaurabham⋅
urovirāji⋅kaustubham
sphuracchrīvatsa⋅lāñchanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

vṛndāvana⋅sunāgaraṁ
vilāsānuga⋅vāsasam
surendragarva⋅mocanaṁ
namāmi nanda⋅nandanam

vrajāṅganā⋅sunāyakaṁ
sadā sukha⋅pradāyakam
jaganmanaḥ⋅pralobhanaṁ
namāmi nanda-nandanam

śrī-nanda-nandanāṣṭakaṁ
paṭhed jaḥ śraddhayānvitaḥ
taredbhavāvdhiṁ dustaraṁ
labhettadaṅghri⋅yugmakam
en_translation:
I bow to the son of Nanda, whose face is beautiful, whose ears are adorned with jeweled earrings, and whose body is smeared with beautiful sandalwood paste.

I bow to the son of Nanda, whose eyes are long and lotus-like, whose hair is adorned with a peacock feather, and who enchants millions of Cupids.

I bow to the son of Nanda, who has a pearl on the tip of His beautiful nose, beautiful rows of teeth, and a body smooth and dark like a fresh rain cloud.

I bow to the son of Nanda, whose hands are beautified by a flute, whose gait shames a regal elephant, and who is adorned with shining yellow garments.

I bow to the son of Nanda, whose beautiful body has three graceful bends, whose nails shine like the moon, and who is adorned with priceless jewels.

I bow to the son of Nanda, who possesses a fragrant bodily scent, who has the Kaustubha jewel shining on His chest, and who is marked with the glittering Śrīvatsa mark.

I bow to the son of Nanda, who is a clever hero of Vṛndāvana, who is adorned with playful garments, and who humbles the pride of the king of demigods.

I bow to the son of Nanda, who is the beautiful leader of the cowherd women, who always bestows happiness, and who enchants the minds of all the worlds.

Whoever recites this eight-verse prayer to the son of Nanda with faith will cross the insurmountable ocean of material existence and attain His lotus feet.
